_comment: YUK-384 — inventory of every knowledge / knowledge_edge topology writer. Each is justified by the PostgreSQL trigger fan-out (drizzle/0071) that dirties the affected hubs in the SAME transaction, so hub-sync correctness is trigger-owned regardless of the writer. A write to either table that is NOT listed here is an UNINVENTORIED_TOPOLOGY_WRITER finding. Reconciliation-cursor writes + the internal-apply GUC are owned solely by hub-sync-reconciliation.ts (no allowlist entry); direct hub_auto_sync applies have no escape hatch.

writers.reason: Dev-only spike script (not runtime); trigger fan-out covers its seed inserts.
One-off maintenance script normalizing knowledge_edge.created_by (non-topology column); inventoried for table-level completeness.
Synthetic dataset seed tooling (not runtime); seed-time writes fire the trigger.
Sole named-primitive owner of knowledge_edge lifecycle (create/update/archive); the topology trigger fans out to every live hub on each edge write.
KC proposal appliers (create/merge/reparent) — knowledge topology mutations covered by the trigger fan-out.
Subject knowledge-tree seed; seed-time inserts fire the trigger and dirty any existing hubs.

Nightly embed backfill writes ONLY non-topology columns (embedding/embed_model/embed_version/embed_content_hash) which the trigger ignores; inventoried for table-level completeness (does not dirty hubs).
Learning-intent KC materialization inserts knowledge; trigger fan-out covers it.
Event-fold projection applier for knowledge nodes (upsert/delete on replay); trigger fan-out covers each applied write.
Event-fold projection applier for knowledge edges (upsert/delete on replay); trigger fan-out covers each applied write.
Proposal-accept edge materialization; trigger fan-out covers the insert.

YUK-452 placement cold-starter admission: inserts the deterministic starter KC under the subject root (identity-keyed, onConflictDoNothing, admission-gated single writer). Topology trigger fan-out applies exactly as for the other inventoried knowledge writers.
Bootstraps the per-subject root knowledge node; trigger fan-out covers the insert.
Subject control-plane knowledge edits; trigger fan-out covers the update.
